blocks:
Sūrat A Ra'ayta

And it is named Sūrat Al-Māʿūn. It is Meccan, and it is said to be Medinan. It consists of seven verses or six. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) أَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي يُكَذِّبُ بِالدِّينِ ** (1) Have you seen him who denies the Retribution? with the recompense (الجزاء). Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Abu Jahl and the disbelievers of Quraysh.

(٢) فَذلِكَ الَّذِي يَدُعُّ الْيَتِيمَ ** (2) That is the one who repulses the orphan, He said: He pushes him away, meaning, from his right (حقّه). It was said that Abu Jahl was the guardian (وصياً) of an orphan, and the orphan came to him naked, asking him for some of his own wealth, but he pushed him away. And Abu Sufyan slaughtered a camel (جزوراً), and an orphan asked him for meat, but he struck him with his staff (عصاه).

(٣) وَلا يَحُضُ عَلى طَعامِ الْمِسْكِينِ ** (3) and does not urge the feeding of the needy. ** وَلا يَحُضُ ** And he does not encourage (ولا يرغب) ** عَلى طَعامِ الْمِسْكِينِ ** due to his lack of belief in the recompense (الجزاء), and for this reason, He connected the sentence to يُكَذِّبُ (denies) using the particle فَاء (fa').

(٤) فَوَيْلٌ لِلْمُصَلِّينَ ** (4) Woe to the worshippers, The فَاء (fa') is consequential (جزائيّة). Meaning, if the lack of concern (عدم المبالاة) for the orphan and the needy is part of denying the religion (تكذيب الدّين), then heedlessness (السّهو) in prayer, which is the pillar (عماد) of the religion, and hypocrisy (الرّياء), and withholding the Zakāh (منع الزكاة), are more deserving of that (woe). And for this reason, He connected the woe (الويل) to it.

(٥)** الَّذِينَ هُمْ عَنْ صَلاتِهِمْ ساهُونَ ** (5) those who are oblivious of their prayers,

They are heedless (غافلون), not caring about it. Al-Qummi said: He meant by it those who abandon it (تاركون), because every person is subject to inadvertence (يسهو) in prayer.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-'Ayyashi, from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse: "Is it the whispering of Satan (وسوسة الشّيطان)?" He said: "No. This happens to everyone, but [it refers to] neglecting it and abandoning the prayer at its earliest time (أول وقتها)."

And al-Qummi narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He said: "It is delaying the prayer past its earliest time (أوّل وقتها) without an excuse (لغير عذر)."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "There is no deed more beloved to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, than the prayer. So, let nothing from the affairs of this world distract you from its times (أوقاتها). For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, censured some people and said:

**الَّذِينَ هُمْ عَنْ صَلاتِهِمْ ساهُونَ ** (5) those who are oblivious of their prayers,

meaning that they are heedless (غافلون) and treat its times (أوقاتها) lightly (استهانوا)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "It is abandoning it (التّرك لها) and being negligent concerning it (التواني عنها)."

And in it (al-Majma') and in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: "It is squandering [the prayer] (التّضييع)."

(٦) الَّذِينَ هُمْ يُراؤُنَ (6) Those who show off,** the people with their prayers so that they may praise them.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He means by them the hypocrites (المنافقين) who do not hope for reward for it (the prayer) if they pray, nor do they fear punishment for it if they abandon it. So they are heedless of it until its time passes. But when they are with the believers, they pray it out of showing off (رياء), and when they are not with them, they do not pray. And this is His saying: الَّذِينَ هُمْ يُراؤُنَ وَيَمْنَعُونَ الْماعُونَ (Those who show off and withhold the assistance).

Al-Qummi: Such as the lamp (السّراج), the fire (النّار), the yeast (الخمير), and similar things that people need. He said: And it (الماعون) is, in another narration: al-Khums (الخمس) and al-Zakat (الزكاة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): It is the obligatory Zakat (الزكاة المفروضة). And in a marfu' (مرفوعاً) narration: It is what people exchange among themselves, such as the bucket (الدّلو), the axe (الفأس), and what is not withheld, like water (الماء) and salt (الملح).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: It is the loan (القرض) you grant, the kindness (المعروف) you perform, and the household goods (متاع البيت) you lend. And Zakat (الزكاة) is part of it. It was said: "We have neighbors who, if we lend them goods, break them and spoil them. Is there any blame (جناح) upon us if we withhold them?" He replied: "No, there is no blame (جناح) upon you if you withhold them when they are like that."

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites the Surah أرأيت الّذي يكذّب بالدّين (Have you seen him who denies the Retribution?) in his obligatory (فرائضه) and supererogatory (نوافله) prayers, Allah will accept his prayer and his fasting, and He will not hold him accountable for what He did in the life of this world (الحياة الدنيا).
